title: Checkout p99 spike (looks like network, actually storage tail + retries)
narrative: checkout-service p99 went to 1.2s. Dashboard suggests network timeouts to payment-service. But deploy history shows a storage config change earlier.

groundTruth.mechanism: fsync/writeback tail latency causes occasional 800ms stalls; checkout retries amplify; symptoms appear as network timeouts
notes: A config change enabled synchronous writes for the local queue (fsync per request).
Disk util is moderate but io latency has heavy tail (await spikes).
When checkout stalls in fsync, upstream hits timeout and retries; the payment-service looks guilty but isn't.
Students should notice: real>>user+sys, iowait, and dmesg/journal hints.

terminal.help:
Try: top -bn1 | vmstat 1 5 | dmesg -T | journalctl -n 20
And compare: real vs user/sys via /usr/bin/time -v (simulated).

commandLibrary.help:
Allowed: top -bn1 | vmstat 1 5 | dmesg -T | journalctl -n 20 | ss -s
No pipes, redirects, or quotes.
